headline: Ukraine confirmation cycle. Composite 50.0 sustained. April 30: Ukraine proposed long-term ceasefire in response to Putin's Victory Day truce suggestion. Russia rejected the framing — Kremlin says Putin would declare unilateral pause for May 9 only. Fundamental disagreement persists: Ukraine wants front-line freeze; Russia demands Donbas withdrawal. April 10: Orthodox Easter 32-hour ceasefire occurred and held. US diplomatic attention has shifted toward Iran. Humanitarian and displacement situation continues at scale. No structural change in window. Watch flag: any sustained ceasefire framework agreement triggers emergency re-queue. Functional band sustained at 50.0.
key_evidence: April 30 2026: Ukraine proposed long-term ceasefire in response to Putin's Victory Day truce suggestion. Kremlin response framed Putin's offer as a unilateral May 9 pause only.
Fundamental disagreement: Ukraine wants front-line freeze; Russia demands Donbas withdrawal as precondition.
April 10 2026: Orthodox Easter 32-hour ceasefire was implemented and held.
US diplomatic attention has shifted toward Iran — reduced bilateral peace mediation capacity in window.
Humanitarian displacement situation continues at scale; no structural resolution in recency window.
Per scanner: Anomaly flag UKRAINE PEACE WINDOW — emergency re-queue protocol activated for any sustained ceasefire announcement around May 9 Victory Day.

evidence.finding: Ukraine proposes long-term ceasefire in response to Putin's Victory Day truce suggestion.
Russia and Ukraine agreed to 32-hour Orthodox Easter ceasefire; held without violation reports.

negativeSignals: Russia rejected Ukraine's long-term ceasefire framing; fundamental Donbas-withdrawal disagreement; reduced US diplomatic mediation capacity; humanitarian displacement at scale.
positiveSignals: Ukraine proactively proposed long-term ceasefire; Orthodox Easter ceasefire held; continued diplomatic engagement capacity.
sector_context: Ukraine remains the only major active-conflict country with consistent positive trajectory signals. The April 30 proposal is the operational positive event. Any actual sustained ceasefire would constitute the largest positive event in Ukraine's benchmark history.
open_actions_resolved: April 30 ceasefire proposal: scored as positive signal, no marginal lift pending implementation
